free_text: The Coupled Fast Reactivity Measurements Facility
(CFRMF)
Coupled Fast Reactivity Measurements
Facility (CFRMF) at the Idaho National Engineering
Laboratory (INEL).
CFRMF at the 6 kW power level which
corresponds to approximately 7.5 x 10**10 n/cm2/sec.
The recommended CFRMF spectrum is shown in Table VI.
Cylindrical proportional counters.
Proton recoil measurements.
235U(n,f) reaction rate uncertainty
The estimated uncertainties on the proton
recoil measurements are +-5% assuming there are no
systematic errors and the possibility of improving
the accuracy of these measurements would be to
discover and correct any systematic errors. The
errors on the 6Li measurements involve the errors on
the 6Li(n,a) cross section over the applied energy
range and in the energy range of triton analysis also
includes the errors on the angular distributions for
the 6Li(n,a)t reaction.
BP

were compiled as the CFRMF spectrum averaged cross
section ratios.
